briefing: Markets pulled back today with NASDAQ down 1.2% and S&P 500 down 0.9%, a risk-off session with no qualifying candidates on the screener. We're holding all 6 existing positions and keeping the $777 cash reserve on the sidelines rather than forcing a buy into weakness.

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' day is when investors broadly pull back from riskier assets like growth stocks, causing indices like the NASDAQ to fall more than safer benchmarks. The smart move is often to avoid forcing new trades and instead protect what you already hold.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「Risk-Off」（避險）是指投資人普遍減少持有風險較高的資產（例如成長股），導致像納斯達克這類指數跌幅大於較穩健的指標。此時明智的做法通常是不勉強進行新交易，而是專注於保護現有的持倉。

market_education.summary_en: Broad indices declined today, with the tech-heavy NASDAQ underperforming the S&P 500, a pattern typical of risk-off sessions where investors rotate out of higher-beta growth names. No stock-specific news was available to explain the move, suggesting it reflects a broader shift in risk appetite rather than a single catalyst. On days like this, sitting on cash and letting existing stop-losses and profit targets work automatically is generally safer than chasing new entries.
market_education.summary_zh: 今日大盤全面回落，科技股較多的納斯達克指數跌幅（-1.2%）大於標普500指數（-0.9%），呈現典型的「risk-off」（避險）格局，資金傾向撤出波動較大的成長股。目前並無具體個股消息可解釋此次下跌，顯示這較可能是市場整體風險偏好轉弱所致，而非單一事件驅動。在這種情況下，持有現金、讓既有的停損停利機制自動運作，通常比勉強進場更為穩健。
